content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Antenna Coverage". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/1253/problem/E
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, the number of antennas. The second line contains n integers, the positions of the antennas. The third line contains n integers, the coverage of each antenna.
content.outputFormat: The minimum number of antennas needed to cover all positions.

solution.approach: Sort the antennas by their positions and then use a greedy algorithm to select the minimum number of antennas needed to cover all positions.

code.Python3:
import sys
n = int(input())
positions = list(map(int, input().split()))
coverages = list(map(int, input().split()))
positions.sort()
coverages.sort()
count = 0
current_position = 0
for i in range(n):
    if positions[i] > current_position:
        count += 1
        current_position = positions[i] + coverages[i]
print(count)
